channel.title: That American Scraper - New Episodes
channel.description: Latest This American Life episodes, newest first.
channel.itunes:author: This American Life
channel.itunes:summary: Latest This American Life episodes, newest first.

item.title: 887: Two Is One, One Is None!
item.description: One family faces the Trump administration’s ban on trans people serving in the military, and responds with a surprising secret weapon.
item.itunes:title: 887: Two Is One, One Is None!
item.itunes:summary: One family faces the Trump administration’s ban on trans people serving in the military, and responds with a surprising secret weapon.

item.title: 886: Blackout
item.description: Since the war began in Iran, we've heard very little from people inside the country — and there's a reason for that. The entire country has been under an internet blackout. We worked with reporters Roxana Saberi and Fatemeh Jamalpour to get voice memos out of the country. Even though it was dangerous and difficult, people wanted to be heard.
item.itunes:title: 886: Blackout
item.itunes:summary: Since the war began in Iran, we've heard very little from people inside the country — and there's a reason for that. The entire country has been under an internet blackout. We worked with reporters Roxana Saberi and Fatemeh Jamalpour to get voice memos out of the country. Even though it was dangerous and difficult, people wanted to be heard.

item.title: 885: Bless This Mess
item.description: At a time when the U.S. government is trying to make American history tidier, we try to learn from the mess. Including the untold, messy story of Paul and Essie Robeson.
item.itunes:title: 885: Bless This Mess
item.itunes:summary: At a time when the U.S. government is trying to make American history tidier, we try to learn from the mess. Including the untold, messy story of Paul and Essie Robeson.

item.title: 884: The Idiot
item.description: M. Gessen returns to our show with a true-crime story that takes place entirely within their own family. This story comes to us from the producers atSerial Productions—who invented the true-crime podcast more than a decade ago—and fromThe New York Times.
item.itunes:title: 884: The Idiot
item.itunes:summary: M. Gessen returns to our show with a true-crime story that takes place entirely within their own family. This story comes to us from the producers atSerial Productions—who invented the true-crime podcast more than a decade ago—and fromThe New York Times.

item.title: 883: Call Your Parents
item.description: In the early days of the radio show, Ira did a series of interviews with his parents that completely changed his relationship with them. This week, he returns to those interviews.
item.itunes:title: 883: Call Your Parents
item.itunes:summary: In the early days of the radio show, Ira did a series of interviews with his parents that completely changed his relationship with them. This week, he returns to those interviews.

item.title: 882: Give a Little Whistle
item.description: Two lawyers who work for ICE step forward and lift the curtain on what is really happening inside our immigration system right now.
item.itunes:title: 882: Give a Little Whistle
item.itunes:summary: Two lawyers who work for ICE step forward and lift the curtain on what is really happening inside our immigration system right now.

item.title: 881: I Want What I Want
item.description: People deciding to do things that most of us do NOT choose to do.
item.itunes:title: 881: I Want What I Want
item.itunes:summary: People deciding to do things that most of us do NOT choose to do.

item.title: 880: What Is Your Emergency?
item.description: 911 calls unlike any we’ve heard before, and other stories about immigration agents sweeping through America.
item.itunes:title: 880: What Is Your Emergency?
item.itunes:summary: 911 calls unlike any we’ve heard before, and other stories about immigration agents sweeping through America.

item.title: 879: A Christian and a Muslim Walk Into a Bar
item.description: When a joke could get you killed, should you say it anyway? A group of Syrian comedians test the limits of their newfound freedom, a year after the fall of the brutal Assad regime.
item.itunes:title: 879: A Christian and a Muslim Walk Into a Bar
item.itunes:summary: When a joke could get you killed, should you say it anyway? A group of Syrian comedians test the limits of their newfound freedom, a year after the fall of the brutal Assad regime.

item.title: 878: New Lore Drop
item.description: People discovering information about their own lives that they did not know, and suddenly everything looks very different.
item.itunes:title: 878: New Lore Drop
item.itunes:summary: People discovering information about their own lives that they did not know, and suddenly everything looks very different.

item.title: 877: The Making Of
item.description: How one block in Portland, Oregon became a movie-set war zone that lots of people think is a real war zone.
item.itunes:title: 877: The Making Of
item.itunes:summary: How one block in Portland, Oregon became a movie-set war zone that lots of people think is a real war zone.

item.title: 876: Bigger Than Me
item.description: When history comes knocking, you have to figure out what to do.
item.itunes:title: 876: Bigger Than Me
item.itunes:summary: When history comes knocking, you have to figure out what to do.

item.title: 875: I Hate Mysteries
item.description: What’s in the box? What’s in the $%&ing box?!?
item.itunes:title: 875: I Hate Mysteries
item.itunes:summary: What’s in the box? What’s in the $%&ing box?!?

item.title: 874: Under One Roof
item.description: What’s great about living in a family is that everyone sees everything differently. Also, that’s what’s awful about living in a family. We go behind closed doors with two families.
item.itunes:title: 874: Under One Roof
item.itunes:summary: What’s great about living in a family is that everyone sees everything differently. Also, that’s what’s awful about living in a family. We go behind closed doors with two families.

item.title: 873: Got You Pegged
item.description: Shalom Auslander goes on vacation with his family, suspects the beloved, chatty old man in the room next door is an imposter, and sets out to prove it. This and other stories about the pitfalls of making snap judgments about others.
item.itunes:title: 873: Got You Pegged
item.itunes:summary: Shalom Auslander goes on vacation with his family, suspects the beloved, chatty old man in the room next door is an imposter, and sets out to prove it. This and other stories about the pitfalls of making snap judgments about others.

item.title: 872: Winners
item.description: America loves winners—now more than ever. But how do you get to a win in 2025 America? We watch someone trying to score a win in a game whose rules are being made up as she plays.
item.itunes:title: 872: Winners
item.itunes:summary: America loves winners—now more than ever. But how do you get to a win in 2025 America? We watch someone trying to score a win in a game whose rules are being made up as she plays.

item.title: 871: The Thing About Things
item.description: Three stories about the strange power inanimate objects can hold over us.
item.itunes:title: 871: The Thing About Things
item.itunes:summary: Three stories about the strange power inanimate objects can hold over us.

item.title: 870: My Other Self
item.description: What happens when people create alternate versions of themselves and release them into the wild?
item.itunes:title: 870: My Other Self
item.itunes:summary: What happens when people create alternate versions of themselves and release them into the wild?

item.title: 869: Harold
item.description: When Zohran Mamdani won the primary race for New York mayor, the Democratic establishment's lukewarm response echoed the treatment of another charismatic, unconventional candidate decades earlier. This week, we bring you the story of Harold Washington, the greatest politician you've probably never heard of, and the backlash that ensued when he became Chicago's first Black mayor.
item.itunes:title: 869: Harold
item.itunes:summary: When Zohran Mamdani won the primary race for New York mayor, the Democratic establishment's lukewarm response echoed the treatment of another charismatic, unconventional candidate decades earlier. This week, we bring you the story of Harold Washington, the greatest politician you've probably never heard of, and the backlash that ensued when he became Chicago's first Black mayor.

item.title: 868: The Hand That Rocks The Gavel
item.description: A group of immigration judges, who almost never speak to the press, describes the dismantling of our immigration court system from the inside.
item.itunes:title: 868: The Hand That Rocks The Gavel
item.itunes:summary: A group of immigration judges, who almost never speak to the press, describes the dismantling of our immigration court system from the inside.

item.title: 867: College Disorientation
item.description: Things are different on college campuses this year. We see inside the drama, with students and staff.
item.itunes:title: 867: College Disorientation
item.itunes:summary: Things are different on college campuses this year. We see inside the drama, with students and staff.

item.title: 866: Watch Out for That Tree
item.description: Small human plans that run into much larger obstacles.
item.itunes:title: 866: Watch Out for That Tree
item.itunes:summary: Small human plans that run into much larger obstacles.

item.title: 865: The Other Territory
item.description: Since October 7th, while the world has focused its attention on Gaza, the Israeli government has tightened the screws on the three million Palestinians in the West Bank in all sorts of dramatic ways. We travel to the West Bank to see these changes in person.
item.itunes:title: 865: The Other Territory
item.itunes:summary: Since October 7th, while the world has focused its attention on Gaza, the Israeli government has tightened the screws on the three million Palestinians in the West Bank in all sorts of dramatic ways. We travel to the West Bank to see these changes in person.

item.title: 864: Chicago Hope
item.description: The story of the most commonly performed surgery, and what goes wrong with it – terribly wrong – 100,000 times a year in the United States. We’re excited to bring you the first episode ofThe Retrievals, Season 2, the new show from longtimeThis American Lifeproducer and editor Susan Burton. It’s from Serial Productions and The New York Times.
item.itunes:title: 864: Chicago Hope
item.itunes:summary: The story of the most commonly performed surgery, and what goes wrong with it – terribly wrong – 100,000 times a year in the United States. We’re excited to bring you the first episode ofThe Retrievals, Season 2, the new show from longtimeThis American Lifeproducer and editor Susan Burton. It’s from Serial Productions and The New York Times.

item.title: 863: Championship Window
item.description: People on a mission to achieve their goals before their window of opportunity closes.
item.itunes:title: 863: Championship Window
item.itunes:summary: People on a mission to achieve their goals before their window of opportunity closes.

item.title: 862: Some Things We Don't Do Anymore
item.description: On his first day in office, President Trump decided to freeze all U.S. foreign aid. Soon after, his administration effectively dissolved USAID—the federal agency that delivers billions in food, medicine, and other aid worldwide. Many of its programs have been canceled. Now, as USAID officially winds down, we try to assess its impact. What was good? What was not so good? We meet people around the world wrestling with these questions and trying to navigate this chaotic moment.
item.itunes:title: 862: Some Things We Don't Do Anymore
item.itunes:summary: On his first day in office, President Trump decided to freeze all U.S. foreign aid. Soon after, his administration effectively dissolved USAID—the federal agency that delivers billions in food, medicine, and other aid worldwide. Many of its programs have been canceled. Now, as USAID officially winds down, we try to assess its impact. What was good? What was not so good? We meet people around the world wrestling with these questions and trying to navigate this chaotic moment.

item.title: 861: Group Chat
item.description: Conversations across a divide: People who are outside a war zone check in with family, friends, and strangers inside.
item.itunes:title: 861: Group Chat
item.itunes:summary: Conversations across a divide: People who are outside a war zone check in with family, friends, and strangers inside.

item.title: 860: Suddenly: A Mirror!
item.description: A show about people who are suddenly confronted with who they are.
item.itunes:title: 860: Suddenly: A Mirror!
item.itunes:summary: A show about people who are suddenly confronted with who they are.

item.title: 859: Chaos Graph
item.description: People immersed in chaos try to solve for what it all adds up to.
item.itunes:title: 859: Chaos Graph
item.itunes:summary: People immersed in chaos try to solve for what it all adds up to.

item.title: 858: How to Tell a Dumb American Story
item.description: A couple devises a strategy to get their daughter's killer prosecuted and to get attention for other Native families.
item.itunes:title: 858: How to Tell a Dumb American Story
item.itunes:summary: A couple devises a strategy to get their daughter's killer prosecuted and to get attention for other Native families.

item.title: 857: Museum of Now
item.description: Artifacts and exhibits of this particular moment we are living through.
item.itunes:title: 857: Museum of Now
item.itunes:summary: Artifacts and exhibits of this particular moment we are living through.

item.title: 856: You’ve Come to the Right Person
item.description: Sometimes, life’s biggest mysteries require one very specific person to answer them.
item.itunes:title: 856: You’ve Come to the Right Person
item.itunes:summary: Sometimes, life’s biggest mysteries require one very specific person to answer them.

item.title: 855: That’s a Weird Thing to Lie About
item.description: Unnecessary and outrageous lies that make you wonder — why lie about that in the first place?
item.itunes:title: 855: That’s a Weird Thing to Lie About
item.itunes:summary: Unnecessary and outrageous lies that make you wonder — why lie about that in the first place?

item.title: 854: Ten Things I Don't Want to Hate About You
item.description: Zach Mack and his dad try to mend a rift between them in a very unusual way.
item.itunes:title: 854: Ten Things I Don't Want to Hate About You
item.itunes:summary: Zach Mack and his dad try to mend a rift between them in a very unusual way.

item.title: 853: Groundhog Day
item.description: People stuck in a loop, trying to find their way out.
item.itunes:title: 853: Groundhog Day
item.itunes:summary: People stuck in a loop, trying to find their way out.

item.title: 852: Pivot Point
item.description: People living in that in-between moment before everything changes.
item.itunes:title: 852: Pivot Point
item.itunes:summary: People living in that in-between moment before everything changes.

item.title: 851: Try a Little Tenderness
item.description: In the new year, stories of people trying a radical approach to solving their problems.
item.itunes:title: 851: Try a Little Tenderness
item.itunes:summary: In the new year, stories of people trying a radical approach to solving their problems.

item.title: 850: If You Want to Destroy My Sweater, Hold This Thread as I Walk Away
item.description: The tiny thing that unravels your world.
item.itunes:title: 850: If You Want to Destroy My Sweater, Hold This Thread as I Walk Away
item.itunes:summary: The tiny thing that unravels your world.

item.title: 849: The Narrator
item.description: Banias is an 8-year-old kid living in Gaza. And she has a story to tell — many stories, in fact.
item.itunes:title: 849: The Narrator
item.itunes:summary: Banias is an 8-year-old kid living in Gaza. And she has a story to tell — many stories, in fact.

item.title: 848: The Official Unofficial Record
item.description: How do you count almost 12 million votes if you’re not the government? This week, we bring you the extraordinary story of hundreds of thousands of Venezuelans who created the only verifiable public record of votes in their presidential election — and other stories of people trying to correct the official record with their own versions.
item.itunes:title: 848: The Official Unofficial Record
item.itunes:summary: How do you count almost 12 million votes if you’re not the government? This week, we bring you the extraordinary story of hundreds of thousands of Venezuelans who created the only verifiable public record of votes in their presidential election — and other stories of people trying to correct the official record with their own versions.

item.title: 847: The Truly Incredible Story of Keiko the Killer Whale
item.description: Keiko was a hugely beloved adventure park attraction. He was also captured in the wild and taken away from his mother when he was just a calf. When Hollywood learned about him, a colossal effort began to un-tame him and send him back to the ocean.
item.itunes:title: 847: The Truly Incredible Story of Keiko the Killer Whale
item.itunes:summary: Keiko was a hugely beloved adventure park attraction. He was also captured in the wild and taken away from his mother when he was just a calf. When Hollywood learned about him, a colossal effort began to un-tame him and send him back to the ocean.

item.title: 846: This Is the Cake We Baked
item.description: With Donald Trump’s victory this week, many people looked at the election results and thought, yeah, this is the country I thought it was. For some people, that was a hopeful thing. For others, kind of the opposite. This week, we talked with people who helped make it happen and some who are looking to what’s next.
item.itunes:title: 846: This Is the Cake We Baked
item.itunes:summary: With Donald Trump’s victory this week, many people looked at the election results and thought, yeah, this is the country I thought it was. For some people, that was a hopeful thing. For others, kind of the opposite. This week, we talked with people who helped make it happen and some who are looking to what’s next.

item.title: 845: A Small Thing That Gives Me a Tiny Shred of Hope
item.description: A wee flame, flickering in the dark.
item.itunes:title: 845: A Small Thing That Gives Me a Tiny Shred of Hope
item.itunes:summary: A wee flame, flickering in the dark.

item.title: 844: This Is the Case of Henry Dee
item.description: Thirteen parole board members decide whether or not one man should be released from prison.
item.itunes:title: 844: This Is the Case of Henry Dee
item.itunes:summary: Thirteen parole board members decide whether or not one man should be released from prison.

item.title: 843: A Little Bit of Power
item.description: So much of the fight for the presidency is coming down to battles for small slices of voters who can help throw swing states to one candidate or another. Abbas Alawieh, a leader in the Uncommitted movement, grapples with how to get his voters the thing they want.
item.itunes:title: 843: A Little Bit of Power
item.itunes:summary: So much of the fight for the presidency is coming down to battles for small slices of voters who can help throw swing states to one candidate or another. Abbas Alawieh, a leader in the Uncommitted movement, grapples with how to get his voters the thing they want.

item.title: 842: 51 Days
item.description: Chen Almog-Goldstein was kidnapped by Hamas along with her three youngest children on October 7, 2023. This week, she tells the story of their life as hostages in Gaza.
item.itunes:title: 842: 51 Days
item.itunes:summary: Chen Almog-Goldstein was kidnapped by Hamas along with her three youngest children on October 7, 2023. This week, she tells the story of their life as hostages in Gaza.

item.title: 841: My Senior Year
item.description: One kid comes to America as an exchange student and commits herself to the senior year experience.
item.itunes:title: 841: My Senior Year
item.itunes:summary: One kid comes to America as an exchange student and commits herself to the senior year experience.

item.title: 840: How Are You Not Seeing This?
item.description: People trying and struggling to see what another person sees.
item.itunes:title: 840: How Are You Not Seeing This?
item.itunes:summary: People trying and struggling to see what another person sees.

item.title: 839: Meet Me at the Fair
item.description: Iowa has three million people and a million come to their State Fair, each with their own goals and dreams for the fair. We hang out with some of them, to see if they get what they hoped for.
item.itunes:title: 839: Meet Me at the Fair
item.itunes:summary: Iowa has three million people and a million come to their State Fair, each with their own goals and dreams for the fair. We hang out with some of them, to see if they get what they hoped for.

item.title: 838: Letters! Actual Letters!
item.description: When the best—and perhaps only—way to say something is to write it down.
item.itunes:title: 838: Letters! Actual Letters!
item.itunes:summary: When the best—and perhaps only—way to say something is to write it down.
